↩️ Reply to OSINT Open Source Intel U.S. President Donald J. Trump says U.S. naval forces are clearing mines from the Strait of Hormuz, following reports that U.S. naval vessels transited the strait this morning. He added that during Operation Epic Fury, 28 Iranian mine-laying boats were destroyed in strikes. Trump: The Fake News Media has lost total credibility, not that they had any to begin with. Because of their massive Trump Derangement Syndrome (Sometimes referred to as TDS!), they love saying that Iran is “winning” when, in fact, everyone knows that they are LOSING, and LOSING BIG! Their Navy is gone, their Air Force is gone, their Anti Aircraft apparatus is nonexistent, Radar is dead, their Missile and Drone Factories have been largely obliterated along with the Missiles and Drones themselves and, most importantly, their longtime “Leaders” are no longer with us, praise be to Allah! The only thing they have going is the threat that a ship may “bunk” into one of their sea mines which, by the way, all 28 of their mine dropper boats are also lying at the bottom of the sea. We’re now starting the process of clearing out the Strait of Hormuz as a favor to Countries all over the World, including China, Japan, South Korea, France, Germany, and many others. Incredibly, they don’t have the Courage or Will to do this work themselves. Very interestingly, however, empty Oil carrying ships from many Nations are all heading to the United States of America to LOAD UP with Oil. Thank you for your attention to this matter! President DONALD J. TRUMP - Open Source Intel tweet

Open Source Intel Two U.S. Navy destroyers, the USS Frank E. Peterson and USS Michael Murphy, have transited the Strait of Hormuz and are now operating in the Persian Gulf. CENTCOM says the mission is to clear IRGC sea mines from the strait. https://twitter.com/Osint613/status/2043022414062268531/photo/1 CENTCOM: U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM) forces began setting conditions for clearing mines in the Strait of Hormuz, April 11, as two U.S. Navy guided-missile destroyers conducted operations. USS Frank E. Peterson (DDG 121) and USS Michael Murphy (DDG 112) transited the Strait of Hormuz and operated in the Arabian Gulf as part of a broader mission to ensure the strait is fully clear of sea mines previously laid by Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps. - Open Source Intel tweet

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇺🇸🇮🇷🇵🇰 NYT: A senior White House official confirmed that trilateral in‑person negotiations in Islamabad are continuing tonight. @wfwitness" ⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 Kan: A senior Israeli official described the ceasefire with Iran as “extremely fragile”, saying Israel is more pessimistic than optimistic. Israel and the US are preparing to return to war if the Islamabad talks fail, with the US reinforcing forces in the Middle East – including fighter jets, munitions and thousands of troops such as the 82nd Airborne Division. Israel has presented a list of demands to Washington: the removal of enriched uranium from Iranian soil, dismantling of the Fordow enrichment facilities, termination of Iran’s nuclear programme, and separation of the Lebanon issue from the Iranian arena. U.S. officials see eye‑to‑eye with Israeli demands but have no trust that Iran will compromise. The next strike on Iran, if talks collapse, would target national infrastructure, energy and oil facilities. @wfwitness

Negotiators say US–Iran talks in Pakistan are stuck, mainly over reopening the Strait of Hormuz. Iran insists it should keep control and charge tolls, rejecting joint control proposals. Talks continue with a working dinner and further discussions planned. Source: FT

🇮🇷 🇵🇰 🇺🇸 "These are the final attempts." IRIB reporter from Islamabad: Two rounds of talks have taken place between Iranian and American delegations. A third round is planned for tonight, the final attempt to see if views can come closer together. The Americans are still showing their "greedy approach" seen in past rounds, both in front of and behind cameras. The shadow of distrust based on American past performance has cast over the talks. Text exchanges between expert delegations and specialized committees are ongoing. Critical moments. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇱🇧 🇱🇧 Kan: The IDF has been instructed to reduce strikes in Lebanon in the coming days, particularly in Beirut, as a confidence‑building measure ahead of direct talks starting on Tuesday in the United States between Israel and Lebanon. Israel will demand that the Lebanese government disarm Hezbollah, with a clear message: either the Lebanese army acts against Hezbollah, or the IDF will continue doing the job itself. An Israeli official said the Lebanese government has a clear interest to act against Hezbollah in Beirut to avoid Israeli strikes there. In the next stage, Israel will allow the Lebanese army to operate south of the Litani River – an area currently under Israeli control and largely evacuated of its residents – but this will take time. @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇱🇧 🇺🇳 🇱🇧 Israeli Channel 12: The IDF has identified that Hezbollah is increasingly using UNIFIL infrastructure and positions to operate against Israeli forces, including firing from or near UNIFIL posts. The Northern Command is striking Hezbollah operatives even when they use UNIFIL assets. An IDF official said: “Hezbollah is exploiting UNIFIL – which knows this and allows terrorism to grow instead of uprooting it. UNIFIL’s mandate ends in 2027; it seems they have decided to just get in our way until then.” UNIFIL responded to the claims: “We are very concerned about attacks carried out both by Hezbollah and by Israeli soldiers from areas near our positions. We call on both sides to lay down their arms and work seriously toward a ceasefire, as there is no military solution to this conflict.” @wfwitness

⚡ 🇫🇷 🇮🇷 Two French Navy mine-hunters and a frigate redeployed from Atlantic ocean to the Mediterranean. They are expected to sail towards Djibouti and ultimately the Hormuz strait for a potential mine-clearing mission. Such mine-clearing mission is only possible with Iran's accord as they cannot operate in contested waters. @wfwitness

The Financial Times reports that Iranian negotiators rejected a proposal for joint administration of the Strait of Hormuz. Iranian television reports that another round of talks is likely to be held tonight or tomorrow.
